source_rationale: TP63 and KRT17 are canonical basal cell markers. The presence of EMT-associated genes (VIM, FN1, CDH2, HMGA2) along with cell cycle regulators (CDKN1A, CDKN2A, CCND1, CCND2) and extracellular matrix genes (COL1A1, LAMB3, LAMC2, TNC) suggests a basal epithelial cell with mesenchymal/proliferative features.

TP63 and KRT17 are canonical basal cell markers. AGER (RAGE) is the most canonical marker of alveolar type I (AT1) pneumocytes. CAV1, CAV2, CLDN18, and EMP2 are also well-established AT1 markers involved in gas exchange membrane formation.
